mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in real spending growth creates a stagflationary demand shock that compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint CPIAUCSL-RSAFS interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates resolving supply chain bottlenecks, which removes cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The positive return signal fails to materialize during post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism produces negative returns when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 235 reflects entrenched inflation that forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports corporate revenue projections, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through fundamental earnings growth.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS levels against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal reverses direction during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising housing starts and flat GDP growth signals sector-specific demand overheating that precedes broader economic cooling, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-GDPC1 interaction term shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, or forward returns remain positive when housing starts accelerate while GDP growth stagnates.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent indicates immediate supply-side contraction that disrupts earnings momentum and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 65 percent of months where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5 percent, or the threshold signal fails walk-forward falsification tests.
